title: In rare sightings, scientists spot blue whales in waters off Martha’s Vineyard
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Scientists from the New England Aquarium spotted blue whales in southern New England waters, near Martha's Vineyard.
Sightings occurred at Northeast Canyons and Seamounts Marine National Monument and 15 miles south of Martha's Vineyard on Feb. 27-28.
These are rare sightings as blue whales typically feed in the Gulf of St. Lawrence during summer, not near New England.

title: One person found, one still missing after fishing boat overturns off Cape Cod
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The Coast Guard recovered one unconscious individual after the Yankee Rose fishing boat capsized near Race Point, Provincetown.
The search continues for a second person missing after the boat overturned three nautical miles northeast of Race Point.
Last week, the Yankee Rose had another emergency with two people hospitalized for carbon monoxide poisoning.

title: Fenway location of Regina Pizzeria has closed
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Regina Pizzeria's Fenway location at 1330 Boylston St. has permanently closed after the lease was not renewed.
The Fenway location opened in 2013 and was part of Regina Pizzeria's expansion within Boston and Massachusetts.
Regina Pizzeria now operates 12 locations across Massachusetts, focusing on quick-service, unlike its iconic North End branch.

title: Mansfield man killed in crash on I-95 in Attleboro, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Chad Askew, 43, of Mansfield died after his Honda Civic struck a tractor-trailer on I-95 North in Attleboro.
The accident occurred near Exit 4 around 5:20 a.m., and Askew was pronounced dead at Sturdy Memorial Hospital.
Authorities are searching for a man in shorts and boots who reportedly fled the scene; investigation is ongoing.

title: Newly released video shows crash that left pickup truck dangling over Zakim Bridge
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Massachusetts DOT released video of a pickup truck crash on Zakim Bridge from February 5.
The truck swerved to avoid a disabled vehicle and was stopped by a snowbank and fence.
No injuries were reported; a tow rotator truck secured the dangling pickup.

title: A Mass. college is buying a local golf course. Here’s why.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Stonehill College is purchasing the 150-acre Easton Country Club in Easton to support athletics and academics.
The club remains semi-private, allowing access to Stonehill's community and local golfers, with current operations unchanged.
The acquisition supports Stonehill's strategic plan and introduces a men's golf program, enhancing student engagement.

title: A deported dad, a city in chaos, and a Tinder sting
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Christina Toledo and her twin sons visited Guatemala where her husband Nexan Asencio Corado was deported.
Chris Muther reported from Puerto Vallarta during citywide fires after a Mexican military operation killed a drug cartel leader.
Patricia Wen covered a failed Tinder sting involving college students attempting a 'To Catch a Predator'-style setup.

title: Boston restaurant hit with brief license suspension after hosting Patriots players’ raucous party
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Estella Restaurant in Boston had its liquor license suspended for one day after hosting a raucous party on Jan. 27.
The party, attended by New England Patriots players, included unauthorized adult entertainment and indoor smoking violations.
Restaurant owner Helder Brandao accepted responsibility, citing player entitlement, but complained of unpaid tabs by players.

title: Netflix acquires Ben Affleck’s AI startup InterPositive
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Netflix acquires InterPositive, an AI tools company co-founded by Ben Affleck, to enhance filmmaking processes.
InterPositive's AI tools aim to protect and expand filmmakers' creative choices, differing from text-prompt video generation models.
Affleck describes AI's role as reducing laborious tasks in filmmaking, aiding visual effects to lower production costs and increase accessibility.

title: Massachusetts earns a D in new online gambling rankings
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts received a D grade in online gambling protections, reflecting a lack of regulation and mandatory limits.
The state's issues include no mandatory loss limits, in-game live betting, and inadequate measures for high-risk patterns.
Massachusetts collected $339.15 million in taxes from sports betting since its legalization in March 2023.
